title: A tamper proof cap
abstract: A tamper proof cap (1) for sealing bottle or bottle like container with threaded neck is disclosed. The cap comprises a safety ring (3) wherein the safety ring comprises an upper ring (5) attached to a detachable lower ring (4) by connection means (6) and having an inner wall (10) formed by four segments (8) attached together by four weak lines (7). When the cap (1) is unscrewed, initially the weak lines (7) tear and deform the lower safety ring (4) into four pieces, and on continued unscrewing the connection means (6) break and release the cap (1) from the neck.
claims: Claims I Claim: 1. A tamper proof cap (1) for sealing bottle or bottle like container with threaded neck comprising a safety ring (3) wherein the safety ring comprises an upper ring (5) attached to a detachable lower ring (4) by connection means (6) and having an inner wall (10) formed by four segments (8) attached together by four weak lines (7). 2. The cap as claimed in claim 1, wherein the cap (1) is made of aluminium or plastic. 3. The cap as claimed in claim 1, wherein the safety ring (3) is made of plastic. 4. The cap as claimed in claim 1, wherein when the cap (1) is unscrewed initially the weak lines (7) tear and deform the lower safety ring (4) into four pieces, and on continued unscrewing the connection means (6) break and release the cap (1) from the neck. 5. The cap as claimed in claim 1, wherein the lower safety ring (4) is attached to upper safety ring (5) by connection means (6). 6. The cap as claimed in claim 1, wherein the lower safety ring (4) is formed by attachment of four segments (8) by four easily breakable weak lines (7). 7. The cap as claimed in claim 1, wherein when the cap (1) is unscrewed, the lower ring (4) deforms and breaks into four pieces demonstrating evidence of tampering of the cap (1). 8. A safety ring (3) for demonstrating evidence of tampering of the cap (1) which comprises a lower safety ring (4) formed by attachment of four segments (8) by four easily breakable weak lines (7), attached to an upper ring (5) by means of connecting means (6); characterised in that when the cap (1) is unscrewed, the easily breakable weak lines (7) connecting the segments (8) to form the lower safety ring (4) tear and breaks the lower ring (4) into four pieces. 9. The safety ring as claimed in claim 9, wherein the upper ring (5) and the lower safety ring (4) are made up of plastic.
